paths.atma_vidya: Path of Self-Knowledge — for identity, self-discovery, who am I
paths.brahma_vidya: Path of Ultimate Reality — for understanding the universe, oneness
paths.moksha_marga: Path of Liberation — for freedom from suffering, transcendence
paths.shanti_patha: Path of Peace — for harmony, blessings, well-being

slokas.sanskrit: ईशावास्यमिदं सर्वं यत्किञ्च जगत्यां जगत्। तेन त्यक्तेन भुञ्जीथा मा गृधः कस्यस्विद्धनम्॥
slokas.transliteration: īśāvāsyam idaṁ sarvaṁ yat kiñca jagatyāṁ jagat | tena tyaktena bhuñjīthā mā gṛdhaḥ kasya svid dhanam ||
slokas.english_meaning: All this — whatever moves in this moving world — is pervaded by the Lord. Enjoy through renunciation. Do not covet, for whose is wealth?
slokas.when_to_use: When greed or possessiveness takes over, or when you need a reminder that nothing truly belongs to anyone.

slokas.sanskrit: यस्तु सर्वाणि भूतान्यात्मन्येवानुपश्यति। सर्वभूतेषु चात्मानं ततो न विजुगुप्सते॥
slokas.transliteration: yas tu sarvāṇi bhūtāny ātmany evānupaśyati | sarva-bhūteṣu cātmānaṁ tato na vijugupsate ||
slokas.english_meaning: One who sees all beings in the Self and the Self in all beings never shrinks from anything in hatred.
slokas.when_to_use: When feeling hatred or revulsion toward others. Seeing yourself in all beings dissolves prejudice.

slokas.sanskrit: केनेषितं पतति प्रेषितं मनः केन प्राणः प्रथमः प्रैति युक्तः। केनेषितां वाचमिमां वदन्ति चक्षुः श्रोत्रं क उ देवो युनक्ति॥
slokas.transliteration: keneṣitaṁ patati preṣitaṁ manaḥ kena prāṇaḥ prathamaḥ praiti yuktaḥ | keneṣitāṁ vācam imāṁ vadanti cakṣuḥ śrotraṁ ka u devo yunakti ||
slokas.english_meaning: By whom directed does the mind go toward its objects? By whom commanded does the first breath move? By whom is this speech impelled that people utter? What god directs the eyes and ears?
slokas.when_to_use: When beginning the journey of self-inquiry. The first question: who or what is the power behind the mind and senses?

slokas.sanskrit: प्रतिबोधविदितं मतममृतत्वं हि विन्दते। आत्मना विन्दते वीर्यं विद्यया विन्दतेऽमृतम्॥
slokas.transliteration: pratibodha-viditaṁ matam amṛtatvaṁ hi vindate | ātmanā vindate vīryaṁ vidyayā vindate 'mṛtam ||
slokas.english_meaning: It is known through every state of awareness. Through such knowledge one attains immortality. Through the Self one gains strength; through knowledge one gains immortality.
slokas.when_to_use: When you feel powerless or mortal. True strength and deathlessness come from knowing the Self.

slokas.sanskrit: अणोरणीयान्महतो महीयानात्मास्य जन्तोर्निहितो गुहायाम्। तमक्रतुः पश्यति वीतशोको धातुप्रसादान्महिमानमात्मनः॥
slokas.transliteration: aṇor aṇīyān mahato mahīyān ātmāsya jantor nihito guhāyām | tam akratuḥ paśyati vīta-śoko dhātu-prasādān mahimānam ātmanaḥ ||
slokas.english_meaning: The Self, smaller than the smallest and greater than the greatest, is hidden in the heart of every creature. One who is free from desire and free from grief sees the majesty of the Self through the grace of the Creator.
slokas.when_to_use: When feeling small or insignificant. The infinite Self dwells within you — greater than the greatest.

slokas.sanskrit: नायमात्मा प्रवचनेन लभ्यो न मेधया न बहुना श्रुतेन। यमेवैष वृणुते तेन लभ्यस्तस्यैष आत्मा विवृणुते तनूं स्वाम्॥
slokas.transliteration: nāyam ātmā pravacanena labhyo na medhayā na bahunā śrutena | yam evaiṣa vṛṇute tena labhyas tasyaiṣa ātmā vivṛṇute tanūṁ svām ||
slokas.english_meaning: This Self cannot be attained by study, nor by intellect, nor by much learning. It is gained only by the one whom the Self chooses. To such a one the Self reveals its own nature.
slokas.when_to_use: When intellectual study alone isn't bringing spiritual clarity. The Self is realized through surrender, not just scholarship.

slokas.sanskrit: उत्तिष्ठत जाग्रत प्राप्य वरान्निबोधत। क्षुरस्य धारा निशिता दुरत्यया दुर्गं पथस्तत्कवयो वदन्ति॥
slokas.transliteration: uttiṣṭhata jāgrata prāpya varān nibodhata | kṣurasya dhārā niśitā duratyayā durgaṁ pathas tat kavayo vadanti ||
slokas.english_meaning: Arise! Awake! Approach the great teachers and learn. The path is as sharp as a razor's edge, difficult to tread — so say the wise.
slokas.when_to_use: When feeling spiritually complacent or lazy. This is the ultimate wake-up call — the path demands alertness and courage.

slokas.sanskrit: ऊर्ध्वमूलोऽवाक्शाख एषोऽश्वत्थः सनातनः। तदेव शुक्रं तद्ब्रह्म तदेवामृतमुच्यते। तस्मिँल्लोकाः श्रिताः सर्वे तदु नात्येति कश्चन॥
slokas.transliteration: ūrdhva-mūlo 'vāk-śākha eṣo 'śvatthaḥ sanātanaḥ | tad eva śukraṁ tad brahma tad evāmṛtam ucyate | tasmiṁl lokāḥ śritāḥ sarve tad u nātyeti kaścana ||
slokas.english_meaning: This eternal tree of existence has its roots above and branches below. That root is the Pure, that is Brahman, that is called the Immortal. All worlds rest in it, and none can go beyond it.
slokas.when_to_use: When pondering the origin and structure of reality. Everything has its source in the one unchanging Brahman.

slokas.sanskrit: न जायते म्रियते वा विपश्चिन्नायं कुतश्चिन्न बभूव कश्चित्। अजो नित्यः शाश्वतोऽयं पुराणो न हन्यते हन्यमाने शरीरे॥
slokas.transliteration: na jāyate mriyate vā vipaścin nāyaṁ kutaścin na babhūva kaścit | ajo nityaḥ śāśvato 'yaṁ purāṇo na hanyate hanyamāne śarīre ||
slokas.english_meaning: The knowing Self is not born, nor does it die. It did not come from anywhere, nor did anything come from it. Unborn, eternal, everlasting, ancient — it is not slain when the body is slain.
slokas.when_to_use: When gripped by the fear of death or mourning a loved one. The soul is eternal — it was never born and can never die.

slokas.sanskrit: यत्तदद्रेश्यमग्राह्यमगोत्रमवर्णमचक्षुःश्रोत्रं तदपाणिपादम्। नित्यं विभुं सर्वगतं सुसूक्ष्मं तदव्ययं यद्भूतयोनिं परिपश्यन्ति धीराः॥
slokas.transliteration: yat tad adreśyam agrāhyam agotram avarṇam acakṣuḥ-śrotraṁ tad apāṇi-pādam | nityaṁ vibhuṁ sarvagataṁ susūkṣmaṁ tad avyayaṁ yad bhūta-yoniṁ paripaśyanti dhīrāḥ ||
slokas.english_meaning: That which is invisible, ungraspable, without origin, without qualities, without eyes or ears, without hands or feet — eternal, all-pervading, omnipresent, extremely subtle — that is the imperishable source of all beings, which the wise perceive.
slokas.when_to_use: When trying to grasp the nature of the ultimate reality. It is beyond all senses yet the source of everything.

slokas.sanskrit: द्वा सुपर्णा सयुजा सखाया समानं वृक्षं परिषस्वजाते। तयोरन्यः पिप्पलं स्वाद्वत्त्यनश्नन्नन्यो अभिचाकशीति॥
slokas.transliteration: dvā suparṇā sayujā sakhāyā samānaṁ vṛkṣaṁ pariṣasvajāte | tayor anyaḥ pippalaṁ svādv atty anaśnann anyo abhicākaśīti ||
slokas.english_meaning: Two birds, inseparable companions, perch on the same tree. One eats the sweet fruit while the other looks on without eating.
slokas.when_to_use: When caught up in experiences and forgetting to observe. You are both the experiencer and the eternal witness within.

slokas.sanskrit: भिद्यते हृदयग्रन्थिश्छिद्यन्ते सर्वसंशयाः। क्षीयन्ते चास्य कर्माणि तस्मिन्दृष्टे परावरे॥
slokas.transliteration: bhidyate hṛdaya-granthiś chidyante sarva-saṁśayāḥ | kṣīyante cāsya karmāṇi tasmin dṛṣṭe parāvare ||
slokas.english_meaning: When that supreme Self is seen, the knot of the heart is loosened, all doubts are dissolved, and all karmas are exhausted.
slokas.when_to_use: When weighed down by doubt and accumulated burdens. Self-realization dissolves all knots of the heart at once.

slokas.sanskrit: ओमित्येतदक्षरमिदं सर्वं तस्योपव्याख्यानं भूतं भवद्भविष्यदिति सर्वमोंकार एव। यच्चान्यत्त्रिकालातीतं तदप्योंकार एव॥
slokas.transliteration: om ity etad akṣaram idaṁ sarvaṁ tasyopavyākhyānaṁ bhūtaṁ bhavad bhaviṣyad iti sarvam oṁkāra eva | yac cānyat tri-kālātītaṁ tad apy oṁkāra eva ||
slokas.english_meaning: Om — this syllable is all this. All that is past, present, and future is truly Om. And whatever else there is beyond the three divisions of time, that too is Om.
slokas.when_to_use: When beginning meditation or seeking connection with the whole of existence. Om encompasses all of reality.

slokas.sanskrit: नान्तःप्रज्ञं न बहिष्प्रज्ञं नोभयतःप्रज्ञं न प्रज्ञानघनं न प्रज्ञं नाप्रज्ञम्। अदृष्टमव्यवहार्यमग्राह्यमलक्षणमचिन्त्यमव्यपदेश्यमेकात्मप्रत्ययसारं प्रपञ्चोपशमं शान्तं शिवमद्वैतं चतुर्थं मन्यन्ते स आत्मा स विज्ञेयः॥
slokas.transliteration: nāntaḥ-prajñaṁ na bahiṣ-prajñaṁ nobhayataḥ-prajñaṁ na prajñāna-ghanaṁ na prajñaṁ nāprajñam | adṛṣṭam avyavahāryam agrāhyam alakṣaṇam acintyam avyapadeśyam ekātma-pratyaya-sāraṁ prapañcopaśamaṁ śāntaṁ śivam advaitaṁ caturthaṁ manyante sa ātmā sa vijñeyaḥ ||
slokas.english_meaning: Not inwardly cognitive, not outwardly cognitive, not cognitive both ways, not a mass of cognition, not cognitive, not non-cognitive. Unseen, beyond all dealings, ungraspable, without characteristics, unthinkable, indescribable — the essence of the awareness of the one Self, the cessation of all phenomena, peaceful, blissful, non-dual. That is the fourth state (Turiya). That is the Self. That is to be known.
slokas.when_to_use: When seeking to understand the deepest nature of consciousness beyond waking, dreaming, and deep sleep.

slokas.sanskrit: सत्यं ज्ञानमनन्तं ब्रह्म। यो वेद निहितं गुहायां परमे व्योमन्। सोऽश्नुते सर्वान्कामान्सह ब्रह्मणा विपश्चितेति॥
slokas.transliteration: satyaṁ jñānam anantaṁ brahma | yo veda nihitaṁ guhāyāṁ parame vyoman | so 'śnute sarvān kāmān saha brahmaṇā vipaściteti ||
slokas.english_meaning: Brahman is Truth, Knowledge, and Infinity. One who knows it as hidden in the cave of the heart and in the highest heaven realizes all desires together with the all-knowing Brahman.
slokas.when_to_use: When seeking the definition of ultimate reality. Brahman is truth, knowledge, and the infinite — and it dwells within you.

slokas.sanskrit: आनन्दो ब्रह्मेति व्यजानात्। आनन्दाद्ध्येव खल्विमानि भूतानि जायन्ते। आनन्देन जातानि जीवन्ति। आनन्दं प्रयन्त्यभिसंविशन्तीति॥
slokas.transliteration: ānando brahmeti vyajānāt | ānandād dhy eva khalv imāni bhūtāni jāyante | ānandena jātāni jīvanti | ānandaṁ prayanty abhisaṁviśantīti ||
slokas.english_meaning: He knew that Bliss is Brahman. From Bliss indeed all these beings are born. By Bliss, once born, they live. Into Bliss they enter at their departure.
slokas.when_to_use: When life feels joyless or purposeless. Your true nature is bliss itself — you came from joy and will return to joy.

slokas.sanskrit: स य एषोऽणिमैतदात्म्यमिदं सर्वं तत्सत्यं स आत्मा तत्त्वमसि श्वेतकेतो॥
slokas.transliteration: sa ya eṣo 'ṇimaitad ātmyam idaṁ sarvaṁ tat satyaṁ sa ātmā tat tvam asi śvetaketo ||
slokas.english_meaning: That which is the subtlest essence — this whole world has that as its Self. That is Reality. That is the Self. That thou art, Shvetaketu.
slokas.when_to_use: When questioning your true identity. You are not the body or mind — you are the ultimate reality itself. Tat Tvam Asi.

slokas.sanskrit: सर्वं खल्विदं ब्रह्म तज्जलानिति शान्त उपासीत॥
slokas.transliteration: sarvaṁ khalv idaṁ brahma tajjalān iti śānta upāsīta ||
slokas.english_meaning: All this is indeed Brahman. From Brahman it is born, in Brahman it dissolves, and by Brahman it is sustained. One should meditate on this with a calm mind.
slokas.when_to_use: When feeling separate from the world or from others. Everything you see, touch, and feel is Brahman — one reality.

slokas.sanskrit: असतो मा सद्गमय। तमसो मा ज्योतिर्गमय। मृत्योर्मा अमृतं गमय॥
slokas.transliteration: asato mā sad gamaya | tamaso mā jyotir gamaya | mṛtyor mā amṛtaṁ gamaya ||
slokas.english_meaning: Lead me from the unreal to the Real. Lead me from darkness to Light. Lead me from death to Immortality.
slokas.when_to_use: When lost in confusion, falsehood, or despair. The most universal prayer for guidance toward truth and light.

slokas.sanskrit: अहं ब्रह्मास्मीति। तस्मात्तत्सर्वमभवत्॥
slokas.transliteration: ahaṁ brahmāsmīti | tasmāt tat sarvam abhavat ||
slokas.english_meaning: I am Brahman. Therefore it became all.
slokas.when_to_use: When feeling worthless or disconnected from your power. The greatest declaration: your true Self is the infinite Brahman.

slokas.sanskrit: आत्मा वा अरे द्रष्टव्यः श्रोतव्यो मन्तव्यो निदिध्यासितव्यो मैत्रेयि। आत्मनो वा अरे दर्शनेन श्रवणेन मत्या विज्ञानेनेदं सर्वं विदितम्॥
slokas.transliteration: ātmā vā are draṣṭavyaḥ śrotavyo mantavyo nididhyāsitavyo maitreyi | ātmano vā are darśanena śravaṇena matyā vijñānenedaṁ sarvaṁ viditam ||
slokas.english_meaning: The Self, my dear Maitreyi, should be realized — should be heard of, reflected on, and meditated upon. By realizing the Self, all this is known.
slokas.when_to_use: When seeking the one thing that makes everything else clear. Know the Self and you know all.

slokas.sanskrit: यथाकारी यथाचारी तथा भवति। साधुकारी साधुर्भवति। पापकारी पापो भवति। पुण्यः पुण्येन कर्मणा भवति पापः पापेन॥
slokas.transliteration: yathākārī yathācārī tathā bhavati | sādhukārī sādhur bhavati | pāpakārī pāpo bhavati | puṇyaḥ puṇyena karmaṇā bhavati pāpaḥ pāpena ||
slokas.english_meaning: As one acts, as one behaves, so does one become. The doer of good becomes good. The doer of evil becomes evil. One becomes virtuous by virtuous action, sinful by sinful action.
slokas.when_to_use: When making choices about your behavior. You become what you repeatedly do — choose wisely.

slokas.sanskrit: एको देवः सर्वभूतेषु गूढः सर्वव्यापी सर्वभूतान्तरात्मा। कर्माध्यक्षः सर्वभूताधिवासः साक्षी चेता केवलो निर्गुणश्च॥
slokas.transliteration: eko devaḥ sarva-bhūteṣu gūḍhaḥ sarva-vyāpī sarva-bhūtāntarātmā | karmādhyakṣaḥ sarva-bhūtādhivāsaḥ sākṣī cetā kevalo nirguṇaś ca ||
slokas.english_meaning: The one God, hidden in all beings, all-pervading, the inner Self of all creatures, the overseer of all actions, dwelling in all beings, the witness, the consciousness, absolute and beyond all qualities.
slokas.when_to_use: When feeling alone or disconnected from the divine. God is hidden within every being — including you.

slokas.sanskrit: वेदाहमेतं पुरुषं महान्तमादित्यवर्णं तमसः परस्तात्। तमेव विदित्वाति मृत्युमेति नान्यः पन्था विद्यतेऽयनाय॥
slokas.transliteration: vedāham etaṁ puruṣaṁ mahāntam āditya-varṇaṁ tamasaḥ parastāt | tam eva viditvāti mṛtyum eti nānyaḥ panthā vidyate 'yanāya ||
slokas.english_meaning: I know this great Being, luminous as the sun, beyond all darkness. Only by knowing Him does one pass beyond death. There is no other path to liberation.
slokas.when_to_use: When feeling that there is no way out of suffering. Knowledge of the supreme Self is the only true path beyond death and darkness.

slokas.sanskrit: ॐ पूर्णमदः पूर्णमिदं पूर्णात्पूर्णमुदच्यते। पूर्णस्य पूर्णमादाय पूर्णमेवावशिष्यते॥
slokas.transliteration: oṁ pūrṇam adaḥ pūrṇam idaṁ pūrṇāt pūrṇam udacyate | pūrṇasya pūrṇam ādāya pūrṇam evāvaśiṣyate ||
slokas.english_meaning: Om. That is whole. This is whole. From the whole, the whole arises. Taking the whole from the whole, the whole alone remains.
slokas.when_to_use: When feeling incomplete or that something is missing from your life. Reality is already whole and complete — and so are you.

slokas.sanskrit: ॐ सह नाववतु। सह नौ भुनक्तु। सह वीर्यं करवावहै। तेजस्विनावधीतमस्तु मा विद्विषावहै। ॐ शान्तिः शान्तिः शान्तिः॥
slokas.transliteration: oṁ saha nāv avatu | saha nau bhunaktu | saha vīryaṁ karavāvahai | tejasvi nāv adhītam astu mā vidviṣāvahai | oṁ śāntiḥ śāntiḥ śāntiḥ ||
slokas.english_meaning: Om. May we both be protected. May we both be nourished. May we work together with great energy. May our study be illuminating. May we not hate each other. Om. Peace, peace, peace.
slokas.when_to_use: When beginning any joint endeavor — study, work, or relationship. A prayer for mutual protection, nourishment, and harmony.

slokas.sanskrit: यो वै भूमा तत्सुखं नाल्पे सुखमस्ति भूमैव सुखं भूमा त्वेव विजिज्ञासितव्य इति॥
slokas.transliteration: yo vai bhūmā tat sukhaṁ nālpe sukham asti bhūmaiva sukhaṁ bhūmā tv eva vijijñāsitavya iti ||
slokas.english_meaning: That which is infinite is happiness. There is no happiness in anything small or finite. The infinite alone is happiness. The infinite alone is worth seeking to know.
slokas.when_to_use: When small achievements leave you empty. Lasting happiness is not in finite things — it is in realizing the infinite.
